definition: Deviation of semantic invariants within substrate logic, leading to loss of meaning stability.
example: Drift occurs when substrate orchestration fails to preserve invariant meaning across layers.

definition: A lawful semantic quantity that remains constant across substrate transformations and orchestration layers.
example: The invariant ensures that meaning is preserved even when substrate geometry changes.

definition: The deterministic coordination of substrate operations to maintain invariant meaning across semantic layers.
example: Orchestration aligns substrate logic and semantic boundaries to prevent drift propagation.

definition: The condition in which meaning remains invariant under lawful substrate transformations and orchestration operations.
example: Semantic stability is achieved when all substrate operations preserve invariant meaning geometry.

definition: The deterministic semantic manifold that hosts meaning-preserving operations and invariant geometry.
example: The substrate defines the lawful geometry within which semantic invariants operate.
